requirement: The FMS shall allow the flight crew to manually input a flight plan of up to 100 waypoints via the Control Display Unit (CDU).
The system shall retrieve latitude, longitude, and magnetic variation for each waypoint from the active Navigation Database.
The LNAV engine shall compute great-circle track and distance between consecutive waypoints in the active flight plan.
The LNAV engine shall calculate continuous lateral cross-track error and output it to the AFCS roll steering channel via ARINC 429 Label 244.
The system shall automatically sequence to the next waypoint when the aircraft enters a 2 NM turn anticipation radius.
The FMS shall support procedure legs defined in ARINC 424 format including TF, CF, DF, AF, RF, and CA leg types.
The system shall allow the crew to insert, delete, or modify waypoints in the active flight plan without interrupting LNAV guidance.
The FMS shall compute and display the magnetic track for each active flight plan leg on the CDU and ND.
The system shall alert the crew when the active flight plan contains fewer than two remaining waypoints before destination.
The LNAV engine shall output desired track angle to the AFCS on every guidance cycle at a minimum rate of 10 Hz.
The VNAV engine shall compute Top of Climb (TOC) and Top of Descent (TOD) based on aircraft performance and lateral flight plan.
The system shall allow the crew to enter Zero Fuel Weight (ZFW) and Cost Index via the CDU performance initialization page.
The VNAV engine shall compute target vertical speed or flight path angle to match the calculated vertical profile.
The VNAV engine shall output vertical guidance commands to the autopilot pitch channel via ARINC 429 Label 245.
The system shall dynamically recompute ETA for all downstream waypoints based on current groundspeed.
The VNAV engine shall inhibit descent guidance whenever the active altitude constraint is more restrictive than the profile altitude.
The system shall notify the crew of VNAV path deviations exceeding 200 ft from the computed profile.
The FMS shall support VNAV speed transitions at defined waypoints from the procedure database.
The system shall compute optimum cruise altitude based on current aircraft weight, Cost Index, and wind data.
The VNAV engine shall limit commanded descent angles to a maximum of 6.0 degrees to remain within structural limits.
The system shall interface with the fuel quantity system at 1 Hz via ARINC 429 to acquire total current fuel weight.
The system shall compute real-time fuel burn rate by summing fuel flow rates from all active ECUs.
The system shall trigger a FUEL DISCREPANCY message when calculated fuel weight deviates more than 500 lbs for over 30 seconds.
The system shall receive independent GPS position from primary and secondary GPS receivers via ARINC 429 Label 310 and 311.
The position module shall compute FMS Best Position using a Kalman filter blending GPS and IRS inputs.
The FMS shall receive barometric altitude from the ADC via ARINC 429 Label 200 at a minimum update rate of 20 Hz.
The system shall receive true airspeed from the ADC via ARINC 429 Label 202 and use it in performance computations.
The FMS shall detect and flag IRS position divergence when two IRS solutions differ by more than 2 NM.
The system shall output FMS computed position to the EFIS symbol generator via ARINC 429 Label 240 and 241.
The FMS shall record all ARINC 429 bus communication faults in the non-volatile maintenance log.
The system shall render the active lateral flight plan including waypoints and curved transitions on the Navigation Display.
The display processor shall project a TOD icon on the ND at the lateral position computed by the VNAV engine.
The system shall display cross-track error as a deviation bar on the PFD lateral deviation scale.
The FMS shall display ETA, fuel at destination, and distance remaining on the CDU progress page.
The system shall generate a CDU scratchpad message for each active FMS advisory condition within 2 seconds of detection.
The FMS shall output active waypoint identifier to the ND via ARINC 429 Label 012 at 1 Hz.
The system shall suppress non-critical display updates when CDU processing load exceeds 80 percent of available CPU budget.
The FMS shall display predicted fuel at alternate airport based on VNAV profile and fuel burn model.
The system shall display wind direction and magnitude at cruise altitude on the CDU wind page.
The FMS shall display a step climb recommendation when a higher altitude yields a fuel saving exceeding the configured threshold.
The FMS shall perform a Power-On Self-Test within 5 seconds of receiving aircraft power to verify RAM, ROM, and processor health.
The system shall maintain a non-volatile maintenance log of all hardware faults and bus failures, accessible via the ground maintenance Ethernet port.
The FMS shall detect navigation database corruption using stored checksums and alert the crew before navigation mode is entered.
The system shall support navigation database updates via a dedicated data-loader interface without interrupting operational software.
The FMS shall output a system health word to the AFCS via ARINC 429 Label 270 on every processing cycle.
The system shall automatically switch to the secondary FMS processor when a primary processor fault is detected within 100 milliseconds.
The FMS shall log a timestamped event record for every mode transition, fault detection, and sensor validity change.
The system shall validate software image integrity using a stored CRC on every power cycle before entering operational mode.
The FMS shall reject any navigation database whose AIRAC cycle identifier does not match the configured valid cycle range.
The system shall provide crew-accessible status page showing current sensor validity, active navigation mode, and maintenance fault count.
The FMS shall enter a reduced-functionality safe mode and inhibit all VNAV and LNAV outputs when internal temperature exceeds 85 degrees Celsius.
The system shall support simultaneous dual independent flight plan editing sessions from captain and first-officer CDUs without cross-contamination.
The FMS shall not modify the active flight plan during the final 1000 ft of approach unless a go-around is initiated.
The system shall inhibit Cost Index computation and default to minimum-fuel mode when fuel quantity falls below the configured emergency fuel threshold.
The FMS shall retain the last valid navigation solution for up to 90 seconds during a complete GPS and IRS blackout before declaring navigation failed.
